quiz.title: Unit 11 Quiz 4, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Advanced Acid-Base Concepts
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Balanced Equations, Amphoteric Substances, Metal Reactivity, Proton Transfer, Weak vs Strong Acids/Bases, Acid-Base Reactions

questions.question: Which equation is correctly balanced?
questions.explanation: HCl + NaOH → NaCl + H₂O is correctly balanced with one H, one Cl, one Na, one O, and two H on each side. The other options have incorrect formulas (HCl₂ and H₂Cl don't exist) or incorrect products (Na₂Cl doesn't exist).

questions.question: Which substance is amphoteric?
questions.explanation: Amphoteric substances can act as both acids and bases. Al(OH)₃ (aluminum hydroxide) is amphoteric — it reacts with both acids and bases. NaOH and KOH are bases only, HCl is an acid only.

questions.question: Which metal will react with dilute acid?
questions.explanation: Zinc is an active metal that reacts with dilute acids to produce hydrogen gas. Gold, copper, and silver are less reactive and do not react with dilute acids.

questions.question: What is the general formula for an acid?
questions.explanation: Acids are substances that donate protons (H⁺). In general, an acid can be represented as H⁺ combined with an anion (e.g., HCl, HNO₃, H₂SO₄).

questions.question: Which substance produces OH⁻ ions in solution?
questions.explanation: KOH (potassium hydroxide) is a base that dissociates in water to produce K⁺ and OH⁻ ions. HNO₃ and HCl produce H⁺, and CO₂ produces carbonic acid.

questions.question: Which reaction involves proton transfer?
questions.explanation: Neutralization reactions involve the transfer of a proton (H⁺) from an acid to a base. This is the defining characteristic of acid-base reactions according to Brønsted-Lowry theory.

questions.question: Which equation shows a weak base reacting with a strong acid?
questions.explanation: NH₃ (ammonia) is a weak base, while HCl is a strong acid. Their reaction produces ammonium chloride. The other equations show strong bases (NaOH, KOH, Ca(OH)₂) reacting with strong acids.

questions.question: Which equation represents the complete neutralization of phosphoric acid?
options: All of the above are possible
questions.explanation: Phosphoric acid (H₃PO₄) is triprotic and can undergo partial or complete neutralization. All three equations are valid depending on the amount of base added, but complete neutralization requires 3 moles of NaOH.

questions.question: The reaction Na₂O + H₂O → 2NaOH shows sodium oxide as:
questions.explanation: Metal oxides react with water to form bases. Na₂O is a metal oxide that produces NaOH, making it a basic oxide. Nonmetal oxides form acids and are called acidic oxides.

questions.question: Which equation shows the reaction of an acid with a base producing a gaseous product?
questions.explanation: 2HCl + Na₂CO₃ → 2NaCl + H₂O + CO₂ shows an acid reacting with a carbonate (which acts as a base) producing carbon dioxide gas. The other options either produce no gas or are not acid-base reactions.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered advanced acid-base concepts! 🧪🔬
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review amphoteric substances and neutralization reactions.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on identifying acids, bases, and their reactions.
scoreMessages.0: Please review acid-base theories, amphoteric substances, and neutralization reactions.
